title: Human antibodies against rabies and uses thereof
abstract: The invention provides human antibodies against rabies and methods of using the same.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1 . An antibody that specifically binds to rabies virus G protein, wherein the antibody comprises a heavy chain variable (VH) domain comprising the following complementarity determining regions (CDRs): (a) a CDR-H1 comprising the amino acid sequence of GFTFSYFAMH (SEQ ID NO: 1 ); (b) a CDR-H2 comprising the amino acid sequence of TIGTGGGTYYADSVKG (SEQ ID NO: 2); and (c) a CDR-H3 comprising the amino acid sequence of CARDNALRSFDWLFYSFDY (SEQ ID NO: 3). 2. The antibody of claim 1 , wherein the VH domain further comprises the following heavy chain variable region framework regions (FRs): (a) an FR-H1 comprising the amino acid sequence of EVQLVQSGGGLVHPGGSLRLSCAGS (SEQ ID NO: 7); (b) an FR-H2 comprising the amino acid sequence of WVRQAPG KG LEW VS (SEQ ID NO: 8); (c) an FR-H3 comprising the amino acid sequence of RFTISRDNAKNSLYLQMNSLRAEDMAVYY (SEQ ID NO: 9); and (d) an FR-H4 comprising the amino acid sequence of WGQGTLVTVSS (SEQ ID NO: 10). 3. The antibody of claim 1 or 2, wherein the VH domain comprises a sequence comprising at least 90% sequence identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 15. 4. The antibody of claim 3, wherein the VH domain comprises the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 15. 5. An antibody that specifically binds to rabies virus G protein, wherein the antibody comprises a light chain variable (VL) domain comprising the following CDRs: (a) a CDR-L1 comprising the amino acid sequence of RASQSISSSYLA (SEQ ID NO: 4); (b) a CDR-L2 comprising the amino acid sequence of GASSRAT (SEQ ID NO: 5); and (c) a CDR-L3 comprising the amino acid sequence of QRYGSSYT (SEQ ID NO: 6). 6. The antibody of claim 5, wherein the VL domain further comprises the following light chain variable region FRs: (a) an FR-L1 comprising the amino acid sequence of EIVLTQSPGTLSLSPGERATLSC (SEQ ID NO: 1 1 ); (b) an FR-L2 comprising the amino acid sequence of WYQQKPGQAPRLLIY (SEQ ID NO: 12); (c) an FR-L3 comprising the amino acid sequence of GIPDRFSGSGSGTDFTLTISRLEPEDFAVYYC (SEQ ID NO: 13); and (d) an FR-L4 comprising the amino acid sequence of FGQGTKLEIK (SEQ ID NO: 14). 7. The antibody of claim 5 or 6, wherein the VL domain comprises a sequence comprising at least 90% sequence identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 16. 8. The antibody of claim 7, wherein the VL domain comprises the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 16. 9. The antibody of any one of claims 1 -8, wherein the antibody comprises the following six CDRs: (a) a CDR-H1 comprising the amino acid sequence of GFTFSYFAMH (SEQ ID NO: 1 ); (b) a CDR-H2 comprising the amino acid sequence of TIGTGGGTYYADSVKG (SEQ ID NO: 2); (c) a CDR-H3 comprising the amino acid sequence of CARDNALRSFDWLFYSFDY (SEQ ID NO: 3); (d) a CDR-L1 comprising the amino acid sequence of RASQSISSSYLA (SEQ ID NO: 4); (e) a CDR-L2 comprising the amino acid sequence of GASSRAT (SEQ ID NO: 5); and (f) a CDR-L3 comprising the amino acid sequence of QRYGSSYT (SEQ ID NO: 6). 10. The antibody of claim 9, wherein the VH domain comprises a sequence comprising at least 90% sequence identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 15 and the VL domain comprises a sequence comprising at least 90% sequence identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 16. 1 1 . The antibody of claim 10, wherein the VH domain comprises the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 15 and the VL domain comprises the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 16. 12. An isolated antibody that competes for binding to rabies virus G protein with the antibody of any one of claims 1 -1 1 . 13. An isolated antibody that binds to the same epitope on rabies virus G protein as the antibody of any one of claims 1 -1 1 . 14. The antibody of any one of claims 1 -13, wherein the antibody is monoclonal, human, humanized, or chimeric. 15. The antibody of any one of claims 1 -14, wherein the antibody is an antibody fragment that binds rabies virus G protein. 16. The antibody of claim 15, wherein the antibody fragment is selected from the group consisting of Fab, Fab'-SH, Fv, scFv, and (Fab')2 fragments. 17. The antibody of any one of claims 1 -14, wherein the antibody is a full-length antibody. 18. The antibody of any one of claims 1 -17, wherein the antibody is an IgG antibody. 19. The antibody of claim 18, wherein the IgG antibody is an lgG1 antibody. 20. The antibody of any one of claims 17-19, wherein the full-length antibody comprises (a) a heavy chain sequence comprising at least 90% sequence identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 

 17; (b) a light chain sequence comprising at least 90% sequence identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 1 8; or (c) a heavy chain sequence as in (a) and a light chain sequence as in (b). 21 . The antibody of claim 20, wherein the full-length antibody comprises (a) a heavy chain sequence comprising the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 17; (b) a light chain sequence comprising the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 18; or (c) a heavy chain sequence as in (a) and a light chain sequence as in (b). 22. The antibody of any one of claims 1 -21 , wherein the antibody binds to an epitope in antigenic site II of rabies virus G protein. 23. The antibody of claim 22, wherein the epitope comprises amino acid residue Glu33. 24. The antibody of claim 23, wherein the epitope further comprises amino acid residue Cys35. 25. A pharmaceutical composition comprising the antibody of any one of claims 1 -24. 26. The pharmaceutical composition of claim 25, further comprising a second antibody that binds to a different epitope of rabies virus G protein. 27. The pharmaceutical composition of claim 26, wherein the second antibody comprises the following six CDRs: (a) a CDR-H1 comprising the amino acid sequence of TYAMH (SEQ ID NO: 19); (b) a CDR-H2 comprising the amino acid sequence of VVSYDGRTKDYADSVKG (SEQ ID NO: 20); (c) a CDR-H3 comprising the amino acid sequence of ERFSGAYFDY (SEQ ID NO: 21 ); (d) a CDR-L1 comprising the amino acid sequence of RASQSVSSYLA (SEQ ID NO: 22); (e) a CDR-L2 comprising the amino acid sequence of DASNRAT (SEQ ID NO: 23); and (f) a CDR-L3 comprising the amino acid sequence of QQRNNWP (SEQ ID NO: 24). 28. The pharmaceutical composition of claim 27, wherein the second antibody comprises (a) a VH domain comprising a sequence having at least 90% sequence identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 25 and (b) a VL domain comprising a sequence having at least 90% sequence identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 26. 29. The pharmaceutical composition of claim 28, wherein the VH domain comprises the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 25 and the VL domain comprises the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 26. 30. The pharmaceutical composition of any one of claims 25-29, further comprising a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier, excipient, or diluent. 31 . The pharmaceutical composition of any one of claims 25-30, wherein the pharmaceutical composition is formulated for treating a subject having a rabies virus disease. 32. The pharmaceutical composition of any one of claims 25-30, wherein the pharmaceutical composition is formulated for treating a subject at risk of developing a rabies virus infection. 33. An isolated nucleic acid encoding the antibody of any one of claims 1 -24. 34. A vector comprising the nucleic acid of claim 33. 35. A host cell comprising the vector of claim 34. 36. The host cell of claim 35, wherein the host cell is a mammalian cell. 37. The host cell of claim 36, wherein the mammalian cell is a Chinese hamster ovary (CHO) cell. 38. The host cell of claim 35, wherein the host cell is a prokaryotic cell. 39. The host cell of claim 38, wherein the prokaryotic cell is E. coli. 40. A method of producing the antibody of any one of claims 1 -24, the method comprising culturing the host cell of claim 35 in a culture medium. 41 . The method of claim 40, wherein the method further comprises recovering the antibody from the host cell or the culture medium. 42. A method of treating a subject having a rabies virus infection comprising administering to the subject a therapeutically effective amount of the antibody of any one of claims 1 -24 or the pharmaceutical composition of any one of claims 25-32, thereby treating the subject. 43. A method of treating a subject at risk of developing a rabies virus infection comprising administering to the subject a therapeutically effective amount of the antibody of any one of claims 1 -24 or the pharmaceutical composition of any one of claims 25-32, thereby treating the subject. 44. The method of claim 42 or 43, wherein the antibody is administered to the subject in a dosage of about 0.001 mg/kg to about 10 mg/kg. 45. The method of claim 44, wherein the antibody is administered to the subject in a dosage of about 0.01 mg/kg to about 10 mg/kg. 46. The method of claim 45, wherein the antibody is administered to the subject in a dosage of about 0.1 mg/kg to about 10 mg/kg. 47. The method of claim 46, wherein the antibody is administered to the subject in a dosage of about 1 mg/kg to about 10 mg/kg. 48. The method of any one of claims 42-47, wherein the subject is administered at least one dose of the antibody or the pharmaceutical composition. 49. The method of claim 48, wherein the subject is administered at least two doses of the antibody or the pharmaceutical composition. 50. The method of claim 49, wherein the subject is administered at least three doses of the antibody or the pharmaceutical composition. 51 . The method of claim 50, wherein the subject is administered at least four doses of the antibody or the pharmaceutical composition. 52. The method of claim 51 , wherein the subject is administered at least five doses of the antibody or the pharmaceutical composition.
